channel.title: IBD LitMonitor
channel.description: Daily IBD literature monitoring - organized by clinical subfield

item.title: Therapeutic Decisions in Inflammatory Bowel Disease: ChatGPT-4.0 Compared to Medical Expertise.
item.description: JGH open : an open access journal of gastroenterology and hepatology - With the increasing prevalence of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) and its complex management, evaluating innovative approaches became essential. Large language models (LLM), such as ChatGPT-4.0, h...

item.title: The Role of Nurse-Patient Mutuality on Self-Care Behaviors in Adults Living with Inflammatory Bowel Disease: A Multicenter Cross-Sectional Study.
item.description: Digestive diseases and sciences - Self-care is a core component of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) management and includes self-care maintenance (behaviors aimed at preserving health and treatment adherence), monitoring (attention...

item.title: A structure-informed deep learning framework for modeling TCR-peptide-HLA interactions.
item.description: bioRxiv : the preprint server for biology - The interaction between T cell receptors (TCRs), peptides, and human leukocyte antigens (HLAs) underlies antigen-specific T cell immunity. Despite substantial advances in peptide- HLA presentation ...

item.title: Volatomics-based biomarkers for non-invasive diagnosis and monitoring of inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Journal of gastroenterology - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), including Crohn's disease and ulcerative colitis, is a chronic disorder that markedly impairs quality of life. Current diagnostic and monitoring tools rely on inva...

item.title: Beyond class effects: moving towards drug-level precision in inflammatory bowel disease therapy.
item.description: Nature reviews. Gastroenterology & hepatology -

item.title: The anti-ulcerative potential of Berberine on the rat model of inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Journal of molecular histology - Ulcerative colitis (UC) is a global inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) and is a chronic mucosal inflammation of the large intestine. UC is accompanied by the increment in the production and release o...

item.title: Maleimide-Modified PEG-PLGA Nanoparticles Facilitate Gut Immune Tolerance via siCD40 Delivery to Dendritic Cells for the Treatment of Inflammatory Bowel Disease.
item.description: ACS applied materials & interfaces - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is characterized by chronic inflammation, driven by immune dysregulation. One of the key contributors to this persistent inflammation is the dysregulation of dendri...

item.title: Transmural improvement may be an acceptable target in patients with Crohns disease.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - Transmural remission is associated with improved long-term outcomes in Crohns disease (CD) but is seldom achieved in clinical practice. Less demanding targets may present reasonable alternatives in...

item.title: A simple MR enterography assessment of Crohn disease activity based on the most affected bowel segment: A retrospective cohort study.
item.description: Medicine - Observational study - This retrospective study aimed to propose and validate a simple magnetic resonance enterography (MRE)-based assessment of Crohn disease (CD) activity based on the most inflamed bowel segment. A tot...

item.title: Potential for anti‑angiogenic therapy targeting the receptor for advanced glycation end products/VEGF axis in ulcerative colitis (Review).
item.description: Molecular medicine reports - Review - Ulcerative colitis (UC), a major form of inflammatory bowel disease, has a global incidence of ~10.6 per 100,000 individuals. The long‑term side effects and dependency issues associated with conven...

item.title: Highly Skewed Immunohistochemical αβ:γδ Ratios for T-Cell Receptors Can Be Detected in Reactive Duodenal Biopsies.
item.description: Archives of pathology & laboratory medicine - T-cell receptors (TCRs) are classified as αβ- and γδ-chain groups. T-cell lymphoma (TCL) has been shown to express either αβ or γδ TCRs. Immunohistochemical stains for TCR-BetaF1 (TCR-BF1) (αβ) and...

item.title: Subclinical atherosclerosis in inflammatory bowel disease: Prevalence, vascular distribution and implications for cardiovascular risk stratification.
item.description: Gastroenterologia y hepatologia - Cardiovascular (CV) risk assessment in patients with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) remains insufficiently explored. This study aimed to assess the role of vascular Doppler ultrasound in refining...

item.title: Pyoderma Gangrenosum of the Eyelid: A Challenging Case.
item.description: The American journal of case reports - Case report - BACKGROUND Pyoderma gangrenosum (PG) involving the eyelid is rare and can closely mimic other differential diagnoses, posing a diagnostic challenge. This case highlights the diagnostic approach and...

item.title: Validation of the Dudley inflammatory bowel symptom questionnaire for assessing gastrointestinal symptom burden in axial spondyloarthritis.
item.description: Rheumatology international - Subclinical and overt inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is frequently observed in patients with axial spondyloarthritis (ax-SpA), traditionally identified through invasive ileocolonoscopic evaluatio...

item.title: Organising pneumonia in ulcerative colitis with primary sclerosing cholangitis: drug-related toxicity or extraintestinal manifestation?
item.description: BMJ case reports - Case report - Pulmonary manifestations of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) are rare but clinically significant. We present a case of a man in his 20s with ulcerative colitis and primary sclerosing cholangitis (P...

item.title: Shared genetic architecture between major depressive disorder and inflammatory bowel disease: Insights from large-scale genome-wide cross-trait analysis.
item.description: Human immunology - Major depressive disorder (MDD) and inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) exhibit clinical associations, yet the cross-disease genetic links and shared mechanisms between them remain unclear. This study...

item.title: Comprehensive overview of the global pharmacogenetic and functional variability in NUDT15.
item.description: Human genomics - Thiopurine medications are widely used for the treatment of acute lymphoblastic leukemia and inflammatory bowel disease. However, thiopurine-induced hemotoxicity occurs in up to 30% of patients, wh...

item.title: Platelet perturbation to ciprofloxacin exacerbates IBD through regulation of HLA DR on CD14⁻CD16⁺ monocytes.
item.description: Internal and emergency medicine - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is a chronic and relapsing disorder influenced by immune dysfunction and other systemic factors. However, the exact pathogenesis of IBD remains incompletely underst...

item.title: Management of Crohn Disease in Adults.
item.description: JAMA - This JAMA Clinical Guidelines Synopsis summarizes the American College of Gastroenterology’s 2025 recommendations on management of Crohn disease in adults.

item.title: "You Can Lead a Horse to Water, but You Can't Make It Drink". Gastroenterology Healthcare Professionals' Perceptions of Reasons for Patient Non-Adherence to Inflammatory Bowel Disease Prescribed Medication: A Qualitative Study.
item.description: Patient preference and adherence - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is commonly treated with medications to induce and maintain remission. Yet many patients do not take treatments as prescribed. Understanding healthcare professional...
